hook: You already know that linear maps respect addition and scalar multiplication — but what happens when you need to measure how two vectors interact, or capture geometric quantities like area and volume algebraically? Standard linear algebra runs out of tools here, leaving you without a way to handle products that depend on multiple inputs simultaneously. This unit builds the richer language that fills that gap, and in doing so reveals why the determinant works the way it does.
concept: The unifying idea is multilinearity: studying mathematical objects that are linear in each of several inputs separately, giving rise to the powerful framework of tensors, bilinear forms, and exterior algebra — collectively called multilinear algebra.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to work with scalar-valued functions that are linear in two vector arguments simultaneously, including how to represent them as matrices and classify them by symmetry and definiteness.
Master the construction of the tensor product $V \otimes W$, understanding how it universally captures bilinear maps and extends the notion of 'combining' vector spaces beyond direct sums.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Exterior Algebra and the Wedge Product (H)
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to build the exterior algebra $\bigwedge V$ using the antisymmetric wedge product $u \wedge v$, capturing oriented area, volume, and higher-dimensional analogues algebraically.

Apply the exterior algebra framework to derive the determinant as the unique alternating multilinear form on an $n$-dimensional space, revealing why its geometric and algebraic properties are inevitable rather than accidental.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Introduction to Tensors in Applied Contexts (H)
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to interpret tensors as they appear in physics, engineering, and data science — including index notation and the idea of covariant versus contravariant components — connecting abstract theory to real-world models.
